message:
fix(abi): route mutex/condvar init to native impl when non-null attrs are provided, reject destroyed attrs with EINVAL

pthread_mutex_init and pthread_cond_init previously always delegated to
the host implementation regardless of whether the caller passed
attributes.  This caused the native validation path (which correctly
detects destroyed attrs) to be bypassed.

Changes:
- Guard host delegation on attr.is_null() so that callers passing
  explicit mutexattr/condattr objects go through the native path where
  attribute validity is checked.
- Merge the nested if-let chains into single let-chain expressions for
  clarity.
- Return EINVAL from pthread_cond_init when condattr_getclock fails
  instead of silently falling back to CLOCK_REALTIME.
- Add mutex_init_rejects_destroyed_attr and
  cond_init_rejects_destroyed_attr integration tests to verify the
  EINVAL return for post-destroy attribute structs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
